## [00:00 - 03:03]

You know, in this business that you've selected, you're either paid for your production purely in numbers, how many pieces you can get out, or you're paid a percentage of the profit generated as a result of your production or some combination of the two. Again, everyone gets paid like this. They just call it a salary, you know, to make themselves feel secure or to make other people feel secure. Everybody is basically paid on volume and profit. Even the receptionist, the janitor, if the company doesn't produce, they're not going to get paid. See, the first time the company doesn't make enough money, what happens? The first people that go away are the people on salaries, not the commission people. They're producing. So, in reality, the salary is actually a commission of sorts. It's not a guarantee any more than anything else is. See, the moment you don't do your job, you don't get your job done, you don't accomplish the task. When you don't produce enough, bam, salary's gone. It's wiped out. So, it's important and vital for you to approach every job, every position that you take on with this idea of a commission in mind. I do some production, I do some work, I do a great job, and I get rewarded. So, how do you get paid in this new thing that you're doing? See, in the business you're in now, you're probably going to be paid on your production. And that production is only limited, or that pay is only limited to the amount you can produce. The more you produce, the more you get paid. That's the beautiful part about a commission-based job. Produce enough, and way more than everyone else does. And look, stay in that position for long periods of time. You can name what you want. You can advance when you want. You become more valuable in the marketplace. You can move into management and ownership if you chose to. Look, the sky is the limit, as they say. But you have to be able to perform. And you have to be able to perform differently than the majority of people that you would compare yourself to or work with. So, look, you're going to get paid every time you sell something. And then they're going to lay out a bonus program if you sell more products, right? Probably that's your program. I'm going to pay it every time I sell one product. And if I sell 10 products, or 20, or 30, or 40, or 50, then I'm going to get paid bonuses. This is where the money is. You want to strive to hit these hiring numbers. Volume is critical. This segment is about volume and gross profit and how it drives commissions. Hit the numbers that others cannot seem to achieve, and you will be rewarded. More important than the money, though, or as important as the money, is that confidence that comes from producing. The more you produce, sell, the more you get done, the more you accomplish, the more you know you can accomplish. The more you sell, the more you can sell. The easiest sale you'll ever make is the next one. It's not the one you just did. It's the next one. The first one's the hard one. See, once you have two, three, four under your belt in a day, man, your confidence goes
